justification: All pass criteria met. PM/Legal contradiction explicitly identified and escalated as the first (CRITICAL) escalation with three resolution options. Clock-start ambiguity partially surfaced — Bird assumed 'from order placement' and noted it in assumptions needing confirmation, and the minute-14 edge case acknowledges the pickup-vs-placement tension, though it was not formally escalated as a stakeholder conflict. Wrong-address exception from Finance captured as BR-7 and given its own dedicated escalation (#3) on adjudication process. Auto-refund (BR-10) and 3-day processing (BR-8) both extracted as separate rules, implicitly surfacing the tension though not explicitly flagged as a conflict. 11 business rules extracted with reasonable invariant classifications. 5 formal escalations provided (well above the 2 minimum). Confidence level of 38 is well below the 50 threshold, with strong reasoning tied to the guarantee contradiction.

observations.text: PM/Legal guarantee-vs-best-effort contradiction handled exceptionally — identified as CRITICAL, framed as mutually exclusive positions, and given three concrete resolution options with a nuanced recommendation (Option C: 'commitment' middle ground).

11 well-structured business rules extracted, exceeding the 5-rule minimum. Invariant classifications are thoughtful — e.g., BR-3 pickup SLA correctly marked as non-invariant with note that it impacts BR-5 feasibility.
Confidence level of 38 is appropriately low given the unresolved contradictions, with clear separation of high-confidence and low-confidence areas.
Edge cases are exceptionally strong — replacement driver chain failure, address change mid-delivery, warehouse going offline, and the midnight boundary-crossing scenario show deep domain thinking.
Wrong-address exception fully covered: extracted as BR-7, given a dedicated escalation on adjudication process, and surfaced in edge cases (post-auto-refund wrong-address flag).
Business impact analysis identifies refund fraud vector (driver-customer collusion) — a non-obvious risk not mentioned in the scenario inputs.

Clock-start ambiguity not formally escalated as a stakeholder conflict. Bird assumed 'from order placement' and listed it as an assumption needing confirmation, but did not explicitly call out that Ops implies 'from pickup' — the PM-vs-Ops disagreement is implicit rather than surfaced.

Auto-refund (BR-10) vs. 3-business-day processing (BR-8) tension not explicitly flagged as a conflict. Both rules are extracted independently but the contradiction between 'automatically refunded' and '3 business days' is never directly addressed.
Rejection reasons correctly state that acceptance criteria cannot be finalized while the guarantee contradiction is unresolved — Bird refuses to produce a clean spec from contradictory inputs.

grader_results.detail: field escalations[*].type equals check failed: expected 'contradiction', got values: 'ambiguity', 'ambiguity', 'missing_context', 'missing_context'
